name: Sales
shortSummary: Own pipeline, run persuasive conversations, and turn business value into revenue momentum.
realityCheck: This is not just being outgoing. Early sales roles usually involve quota pressure, repetition, and resilience after rejection.
stretchTendency: Usually reachable early if you genuinely enjoy persuasion, outreach, and direct ownership.
detail: Business-side sales roles in tech center on discovery, value framing, objection handling, and moving buying conversations forward. Strong fits usually enjoy fast-paced communication, ownership, and commercial pressure.

name: Sales Engineering
shortSummary: Translate product capabilities into clear customer-facing solutions and handle technical credibility in the sales cycle.
realityCheck: This role sounds glamorous from the outside, but companies usually expect real technical fluency, product depth, and live-demo composure.
stretchTendency: Often a stretch without strong technical comfort, presentation confidence, and technical proof points.
detail: Sales Engineering sits between product depth and commercial storytelling. The strongest fits can explain systems clearly, handle technical questions under pressure, and connect product capabilities to customer outcomes.

name: Project / Program Management
shortSummary: Create structure, coordinate work across teams, and keep execution moving from plan to delivery.
realityCheck: Good PMs are not meeting schedulers. They create operating clarity, manage risk, and maintain credibility with busy stakeholders.
stretchTendency: Reachable when process, execution, and collaboration are already strong. TPM-leaning versions need extra technical credibility.
detail: This family rewards people who naturally build order, track dependencies, and follow through. Technical Program Management is represented here as a guarded variant for candidates who pair that execution strength with stronger systems and technical fluency.

name: Product Management
shortSummary: Turn customer insight, business tradeoffs, and cross-functional judgment into product direction.
realityCheck: Entry-level PM is often over-romanticized. Most teams want evidence that you can synthesize users, data, ambiguity, and stakeholder tradeoffs before they trust you with product calls.
stretchTendency: Deliberately hard to recommend without strong customer, analytical, ownership, and cross-functional evidence.
detail: Product Management is about decision quality under ambiguity, not just ideation. Strong fits can learn from customers, frame tradeoffs, and create momentum across design, engineering, and business partners.

name: Finance
shortSummary: Use numbers, models, and operating metrics to support planning, analysis, and business decisions.
realityCheck: Finance roles demand comfort with metrics, disciplined thinking, and credibility with assumptions. It is not just 'business strategy with spreadsheets.'
stretchTendency: Reachable when quantitative comfort and analytical discipline are already visible.
detail: Finance roles in tech often support forecasting, planning, KPI tracking, and scenario analysis. Candidates who like models, metrics, and decision support usually show the strongest fit.

name: Accounting
shortSummary: Protect financial accuracy, controls, and reporting discipline across recurring business operations.
realityCheck: Accounting is less about broad strategy and more about consistency, controls, and accuracy when details really matter.
stretchTendency: Best when detail orientation and quantitative comfort are clearly stronger than average.
detail: Accounting roles reward people who are reliable with detail, disciplined with process, and comfortable working through reconciliations, records, and controls that have little room for sloppiness.

name: Customer Success
shortSummary: Help customers realize value, sustain trust, and navigate adoption, risk, and growth after the sale.
realityCheck: Customer Success is not passive account management. It mixes relationship building with problem solving, commercial judgment, and calm follow-through.
stretchTendency: Usually reachable if empathy, communication, and cross-functional follow-through are real strengths.
detail: Customer Success sits at the intersection of relationship management, product adoption, and value realization. Strong fits build trust, understand pain points, and coordinate internally to keep customers on track.

name: Business Systems Analyst
shortSummary: Translate business needs into better workflows, requirements, and system improvements.
realityCheck: This is a translator role, not just documentation. Credibility comes from understanding both the business process and how the system behaves.
stretchTendency: Strong fits need systems, process, analytical, and technical signals together, not in isolation.
detail: Business Systems Analysts connect workflows, requirements, tooling, and operating realities. Strong fits enjoy diagnosing process pain, improving configuration, and making business systems easier to use.
